trigger.reason: Google DeepMind's Gemini 3.5 Flash agent release with autonomous task execution, combined with Gemini Omni multimodal video generation and Search transformation to AI-powered agents, represents the most market-moving trigger event — likely to trigger sympathy-fade selling across low-code/no-code tools, edtech, customer-service software, and content creation platforms.

primary_event.headline: Gemini 3.5 Flash: Google bets next AI wave on agents, not chatbots
primary_event.summary: Google launched Gemini 3.5 Flash at Google I/O, its most powerful coding and agentic AI model capable of autonomously executing complex tasks and building software from scratch. Combined with Gemini Omni (multimodal video generation from text/image/audio) and the transformation of Google Search into an AI-powered agentic experience, this represents a major capability expansion into autonomous software development and task automation.

secondary_events.headline: Gemini Omni: multimodal video generation from text, images, and audio
Google Search transformed into AI-powered conversational agent experience
AI Studio: generate native Android apps in minutes

discovery.trigger_acknowledgment: Analyzed Google DeepMind's Gemini 3.5 Flash agentic AI release (with Gemini Omni and AI-powered Search transformation) for sympathy-fade impact across cyber and semis names.
discovery.run_summary: The trigger flags low-code/edtech/content-creation as the at-risk sectors, but today's basket is dominated by cybersecurity and semis names with little direct overlap. TENB and VRNS are actually UP on the day (cyber benefits as AI-generated exploits proliferate and as enterprises need data governance under agents), so no sympathy-fade BUY is needed. PLAB is down on the day in a broader tech-selloff context but the panic logic is mechanically backwards — photomasks are a direct AI-capex beneficiary.

discoveries.name: Photronics, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors discuss dependency on the microelectronics industry, capex cycles, customer concentration, and tariffs — no AI-substitution language. Photomasks are a required input for every IC including AI accelerators; Risk Factors note demand depends on design activity, which AI-accelerator proliferation increases.
discoveries.thesis: Google's agentic AI release expands demand for AI silicon, which mechanically expands photomask demand. PLAB selling off into AI-lab news is backwards — they are a picks-and-shovels beneficiary, not a victim. The down move is pure thematic 'tech-selloff' contagion with no business-model overlap.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued AI-capex commentary from foundry customers (TSMC, Samsung, SMIC) in coming quarters; PLAB's leading-edge mix expanding; design-release counts rising.
discoveries.what_kills: An actual AI-capex slowdown signal from foundry customers; PLAB customer-concentration shock (top-2 customers are 29% of revenue); China geopolitical escalation hitting their two Chinese facilities; a 10-Q segment-level miss not yet visible (Q is essentially blank, referring back to 10-K).
discoveries.what_to_learn: Picks-and-shovels semis names sometimes sell off on AI-lab news on pure 'tech is down' reflex despite being direct beneficiaries. Track whether this pattern resolves within days or weeks — a clean teaching example of mechanically backwards sympathy fade.
discoveries.catalyst: Gemini 3.5 Flash: Google bets next AI wave on agents, not chatbots
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: PLAB is in the 'AI-adjacent' basket via the semiconductor supply chain; on AI-lab agent-release days, the broader semi tape can sell off on 'AI capex peak' narrative even when the name is a direct beneficiary.
research_pointers: Check TSMC/Samsung/Intel recent capex commentary for leading-edge node activity
Look at PLAB's most recent earnings call for AI-related design-activity commentary
Verify the 5-day drawdown isn't an earnings-related move (10-Q filed 2026-03-11; check for any post-Q guidance)
Cross-check with peer photomask suppliers (Hoya, DNP) for any industry-specific weakness signal

skipped.reason: Stock is UP +9.21% today and +20.18% over 5 days — no sympathy-fade panic to grade; cyber names are catching a bid as AI-generated exploits validate vuln-mgmt demand.

Stock is UP +3.44% today — no panic to grade; 10-Q explicitly cites AI-driven demand for data security (machine identities, agent governance) as a tailwind, confirming the curator's 'AI beneficiary' read but no actionable sympathy-fade setup.
